issues.symptoms: Brown lesions with yellow halos on leaves, spreading in wet weather
issues.cause: Fungal infection (Bacterial/Fungal leaf blight), worsened by excess nitrogen and standing water
issues.remedy: Improve field drainage, avoid excess nitrogen fertilizer, apply a copper-based fungicide spray if available, use resistant seed varieties next season

issues.symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves, leaves curling and yellowing
issues.cause: Fungal infection favored by high humidity and poor air circulation
issues.remedy: Space plants for airflow, spray diluted neem oil solution weekly, remove and destroy heavily infected leaves

issues.symptoms: Uniform yellowing starting from older/lower leaves upward
issues.cause: Often nitrogen deficiency, sometimes overwatering leading to root stress
issues.remedy: Apply a balanced NPK or urea top-dressing in small doses, check soil isn't waterlogged, retest after 5-7 days

issues.symptoms: Curled leaves, sticky residue, clusters of small insects on undersides of leaves
issues.cause: Aphid colonization, often worse in dry warm weather
issues.remedy: Spray a soap-water solution (mild dish soap + water) directly on aphids, introduce ladybird beetles if possible, avoid broad-spectrum pesticides that kill natural predators

issues.symptoms: Dark sunken spots on fruit, sometimes with concentric rings
issues.cause: Fungal infection (e.g., anthracnose), spread by water splash and high humidity
issues.remedy: Remove and destroy affected fruit, avoid overhead watering, apply a copper-based fungicide, mulch to reduce soil splash

issues.symptoms: Plants shorter than expected for growth stage, pale green color
issues.cause: Often phosphorus deficiency or compacted soil restricting root growth
issues.remedy: Loosen soil around root zone, apply DAP or a phosphorus-rich fertilizer at recommended dose, ensure adequate but not excess watering

issues.symptoms: Plant wilts even though soil is moist, sometimes one-sided
issues.cause: Possible bacterial wilt or root damage from waterlogging/pests
issues.remedy: Check roots for rot or grubs, improve drainage, remove severely affected plants to prevent spread, avoid replanting brinjal in same soil next season

issues.symptoms: Leaves curl upward and become leathery, stunted new growth
issues.remedy: Control whitefly population with yellow sticky traps or neem spray, remove infected plants, avoid planting near other virus-prone crops

issues.symptoms: Plant yellows and collapses, roots appear dark and mushy when pulled
issues.cause: Fungal root rot from waterlogged or poorly drained soil
issues.remedy: Improve drainage immediately, avoid over-irrigation, apply a soil fungicide drench if available, rotate crops next season

issues.symptoms: Leaf tips and edges turning brown/crispy shortly after fertilizing
issues.cause: Over-fertilization, especially with high-nitrogen chemical fertilizers
issues.remedy: Flush soil with plain water, halt fertilizer application for 1-2 weeks, resume at half the previous dose

issues.symptoms: Dark concentric ring spots on older leaves, yellowing around spots
issues.cause: Fungal infection (Alternaria solani), worse in warm humid weather with plant stress
issues.remedy: Remove and destroy infected leaves, avoid overhead watering, apply a copper-based or mancozeb fungicide, rotate crops away from potato/tomato next season
